data.title: Cloud-Based Infrastructure
data.description: Adobe's platform leverages cloud computing to deliver scalable services and applications.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes public cloud providers for elastic scaling and high availability.

data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Services are broken down into microservices to promote decoupling and ease of deployment.
data.technical_spec: Employs containerization technologies like Docker and orchestration via Kubernetes.

data.title: API-Driven Approach
data.description: APIs serve as the communication bridge between different Adobe services and third-party integrations.
data.technical_spec: RESTful APIs with OAuth 2.0 for authentication and authorization.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: A global CDN is used to deliver content with low latency and high availability.
data.technical_spec: Edge servers positioned globally to cache Adobe content and reduce load times.

data.title: Database Management Systems
data.description: Diverse DBMS solutions are used across Adobe's services, including SQL and NoSQL databases.
data.technical_spec: Relational databases for transactional data, NoSQL databases for unstructured data.
